# BUG: a same-line trailing comment on the declaration PRECEDING a match
# was stolen by the sweep: the interval (prevDeclEnd, decl.End()] starts
# right after the previous decl's End(), and a trailing `// note` on that
# decl's last line ends inside it. The comment belongs to the unmoved
# decl and must stay in the source — and never appear in the sink.

# move: trailing comment stays attached to var x in the source.
exec sflit -source a.go -sink b.go -regex '^Matched$' -move
cmp a.go expected_a.go
cmp b.go expected_b.go

# copy: source untouched on disk, sink free of the stolen comment.
exec sflit -source c.go -sink d.go -regex '^Copied$'
cmp c.go c_orig.go
cmp d.go expected_d.go

# move: previous decl is a FuncDecl with a trailing `} // note` comment.
exec sflit -source e.go -sink f.go -regex '^Moved$' -move
cmp e.go expected_e.go
cmp f.go expected_f.go

# move: first decl matched — package clause trailing comment stays.
exec sflit -source g.go -sink h.go -regex '^First$' -move
cmp g.go expected_g.go
cmp h.go expected_h.go

-- a.go --
package p

var x = 1 // important note about x

func Matched() {}
-- expected_a.go --
package p

var x = 1 // important note about x
-- expected_b.go --
package p

func Matched() {}
-- c.go --
package p

var y = 2 // note about y

func Copied() {}
-- c_orig.go --
package p

var y = 2 // note about y

func Copied() {}
-- expected_d.go --
package p

func Copied() {}
-- e.go --
package p

func Prev() {} // note on Prev

func Moved() {}
-- expected_e.go --
package p

func Prev() {} // note on Prev
-- expected_f.go --
package p

func Moved() {}
-- g.go --
package p // clause note

func First() {}

func Keep() {}
-- expected_g.go --
package p // clause note

func Keep() {}
-- expected_h.go --
package p

func First() {}
